# Post-installation configuration

<Note>

This page covers one-time first-run bootstrap tasks after deployment. For ongoing operator governance and configuration-as-code workflows, see the Administration Guide: [Configuration as code](/validated-designs/nomad/administration-guide/configuration-as-code).

</Note>

After deploying and bootstrapping Nomad Enterprise, follow these steps to configure the servers.

1. <a href="https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/secure/traffic" target="_blank">Enable mTLS and gossip encryption</a>
1. [Enable and configure access control lists and OIDC for RBAC](/validated-designs/nomad/administration-guide/identity-access-management)
1. [Set up automated upgrades to ensure Nomad cluster stays up-to-date with the latest features and security patches](/validated-designs/nomad/administration-guide/upgrade-procedures)
1. [Implement a robust backup strategy to protect your Nomad cluster's state and configuration](/validated-designs/nomad/administration-guide/backup-and-restore-operations)
1. <a href="https://developer.hashicorp.com/nomad/docs/deploy/clusters/reverse-proxy-ui" target="_blank">Load balance and reverse proxy for Nomad's web UI</a>
1. [Use Terraform to manage Nomad configuration as code, enabling version control and reproducibility](/validated-designs/nomad/administration-guide/configuration-as-code#configure-nomad-as-code)
